title: 🧩subworkflow - jira loop until issue exists
description:
Subworkflow to repeatedly search Jira until an issue exists and can be returned to the parent workflow.

This is useful when another system creates the Jira issue asynchronously and the current automation needs to wait until the issue becomes searchable via JQL.

The workflow intentionally fails the validation step when no issue is found yet so that a parent workflow or task retry configuration can loop and try again later.

Parameters:

search_query: (Required) JQL query used to search for the Jira issue
dynatrace_jira_connection: (Required) Name of the Dynatrace Jira connection to use
guide:
## 🔁 **Subworkflow to Loop Until a Jira Issue Exists**

🔎 This subworkflow repeatedly searches Jira using a provided **JQL query** until a matching issue exists and can be returned to the calling workflow.

It is useful in automations where a Jira issue is created asynchronously and follow-up steps should only continue once the issue is actually available through search.

---

### 🎯 **Use Case**

A common use case is waiting for an issue that was triggered by a previous automation step, integration, or external process.

If the issue is not found yet, the workflow deliberately fails the validation step so that **retry logic in the parent workflow or task configuration** can restart the check after a delay.

---

### ⚙️ **Parameters**

- **search_query** (Required): 🧾 The JQL query used to search for the target Jira issue.
- **dynatrace_jira_connection** (Required): 🔑 The name of the Dynatrace Jira connection used for authentication.

---

### 🧠 **Workflow Logic**

- 🔍 Executes the provided JQL query against Jira
- 📭 Checks whether any matching issues were returned
- ❌ If no issue exists yet, the JavaScript validation task fails on purpose
- 🔁 This allows parent-level retry logic to re-run the workflow until the issue becomes available
- ✅ Once an issue is found, the search result is returned to downstream steps

---

### 📌 **Important Note**

This subworkflow itself performs the lookup and the intentional failure. To achieve the actual wait-loop behavior, use it together with a **retry-enabled parent workflow or task** that re-invokes it until it succeeds.

---

search-for-existing-issue.name: search-for-existing-issue
search-for-existing-issue.description: Execute JQL queries to fetch issues from Jira
